name: Dark Matter Stash
tips: Best played late when you already have multiple Mages on the board with high Dark Matter stacks — the buff scales multiplicatively with both Mage count and DM.

Discard this with Shady Trade Deal for a 2-for-1: Stash's on-discard +1 DM lands immediately as the discard cost is paid, then Shady's own +1 DM resolves after the react window. You net +2 DM per Mage for just 1 mana — and Stash's DM sticks even if Shady itself gets negated.
Pair with Ratchanter — the Rats he conjures don't benefit, but Ratchanter himself is a Mage and receives the full buff.

At 5 mana this competes with Erebus for your mana. If your DM is high enough, Erebus may be cheaper and put a body on board. Use Stash when you need to buff existing Mages rather than deploy a new one.
Sometimes it's better to hold this card as discard fodder than to play it — the on-discard effect costs 0 mana and still grants DM.
rulings: When played, each friendly Mage on the board gains attack and health equal to that Mage's OWN Dark Matter stacks (per-Mage, not a pooled player total), then gains +1 Dark Matter.
The buff is applied first, then the Dark Matter is granted. A Mage with 3 Dark Matter gets +3🗡️/+3🤍, then goes to 4 Dark Matter.
If discarded from hand (e.g. by Shady Trade Deal), the on-discard effect triggers: each friendly Mage gains +1 Dark Matter. The buff effects do not trigger on discard.

Only Mages on the board when the spell resolves — after the react window closes — receive the buff. A Mage removed during the react window gets nothing, and Mages played later do not retroactively benefit.
